subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Master’s Degree in Information Technology
Completed undergraduate studies in Computer Science
Has taken specialized courses in network administration and cybersecurity
Earned certifications such as CompTIA Network+ and Cisco CCNA
Attended workshops on emerging network technologies during graduate studies

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Job title: Computer Network Support Specialist
Responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting, and supporting company network infrastructure
Acts as the primary contact for employee technical issues related to connectivity
Assists with installation and configuration of network devices and user accounts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the technology sector
Supports IT infrastructure for a mid-sized company
Industry focus includes business services and internal IT operations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Employed at a company with approximately 200-500 employees
Organization has a dedicated IT department with 5-10 technical staff
Company operates a hybrid environment with both onsite and remote employees
Collaborates with multiple departments to ensure network reliability

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Company operates in the B2B services segment
Provides internal technological support rather than consumer-facing products
Focuses on ensuring seamless business operations through robust IT infrastructure

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 6-10 years of experience as a Computer Network Support Specialist
Joined current organization soon after completing graduate studies
Has grown responsibilities steadily over tenure in the role

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Considered mid-level in the IT support career track
Serves as a go-to resource for complex technical issues within the team
Mentors junior support staff and interns
Participates in departmental decision-making about network upgrades

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Monitors network systems and server health using tools like Wireshark
Troubleshoots connectivity and access issues for employees
Performs software updates and security patch installations
Documents incidents and resolutions for future reference
Guides employees through technical issues via remote support tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends about 40% of time on monitoring and maintenance tasks
Troubleshooting and user support take up roughly 35% of daily activity
Approximately 15% dedicated to software updates and patch management
Allocates remaining time to documentation and device setup

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Has discretion to resolve most network and user issues independently
Consults with team leads or management for major system changes or incidents
Can select troubleshooting methods and tools as needed
Makes recommendations on hardware and software upgrades

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Quickly identifies and resolves common Wi-Fi and printer connectivity issues
Efficient at performing routine software updates and security patches
Comfortable using remote desktop tools for basic troubleshooting
Skilled at guiding users through straightforward technical problems

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Diagnosing intermittent or multi-user network outages
Resolving issues caused by misconfigured switches or routers
Handling complex security incidents or potential breaches
Integrating new devices or systems into the existing network architecture

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Sorting and assigning incoming support tickets
Generating and distributing routine network status reports
Monitoring system logs for common error patterns
Applying standardized security patches across multiple endpoints

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys troubleshooting unique or complex technical problems
Finds satisfaction in successfully restoring network connectivity after outages
Considers repetitive ticket triage and routine updates less engaging
Prefers user-facing problem-solving over administrative reporting tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Dealing with vague or incomplete user reports of technical issues
Switching between multiple, non-integrated monitoring and ticketing systems
Repeating basic troubleshooting steps for recurring problems
Limited by lack of proactive alerts for emerging network issues

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Feels most productive when working on tasks that align with technical strengths
Higher engagement is often linked to more challenging problem-solving work
Routine, low-skill tasks are completed efficiently but with less enthusiasm
Continuous learning and upskilling contribute to both enjoyment and effectiveness

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Enthusiastic about exploring new IT and network tools
Views learning as essential for staying effective in the role
Actively seeks out information on emerging tech trends

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to updates in familiar platforms like Windows and Linux
Comfortable incorporating new monitoring or remote access tools into routine
Rarely needs extended onboarding to become fluent in new IT solutions

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Regularly dedicates time after work for online courses or tutorials
Participates in company-sponsored training sessions when available
Believes ongoing training is crucial for career development

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by desire to solve complex problems more efficiently
Sometimes workload peaks limit time available for deep learning
Prefers training that is directly relevant to current job tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers often share tips and encourage trying new technologies
Management supports adoption of tools that improve workflow
Team culture values continuous improvement and tech adoption

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses TeamViewer and AnyDesk for remote desktop support
Employs Wireshark for network traffic monitoring and diagnostics
Relies on Microsoft Teams for internal communication and collaboration
Uses Jira to track support requests and incident tickets

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Command-line utilities like ipconfig, ping, and traceroute for diagnostics
Windows Defender for antivirus and endpoint security
Active Directory for managing user accounts and permissions
Physical tools such as cable testers for hardware troubleshooting

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient with Windows and Linux operating systems
Experienced with Cisco networking equipment
Familiar with enterprise firewall and VPN solutions
Understands standards like TCP/IP, DHCP, and DNS

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally encounters compatibility issues between remote access tools and endpoint security software
Ticketing and monitoring platforms lack seamless integration, requiring manual updates
Some legacy hardware causes challenges when interfacing with modern management systems
Periodically needs to export data manually between systems for reporting

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic AI and machine learning terms like model, training, and inference
Regularly uses large language models (LLMs) in daily workflow
Has explored AI-driven diagnostic and recommendation systems
Follows industry news on AI trends relevant to IT support

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGPT and similar LLMs for troubleshooting advice and scripting help
Leverages built-in AI features in Microsoft Teams for meeting summaries
Occasionally tests AI-powered ticket triage in Jira
Experiments with AI-driven anomaly detection tools in network monitoring

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Accesses LLMs daily to generate solutions for uncommon network issues
Uses AI to draft responses for repeat support queries
Employs AI chatbots to assist with first-level employee questions
Applies AI tools for log file analysis and pattern detection

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: AI chatbots have successfully resolved simple password reset requests, freeing up time
LLMs provided helpful diagnostic scripts that reduced troubleshooting time
Occasionally received AI-generated suggestions that were outdated or contextually irrelevant
Learned to always verify AI recommendations before implementation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Company offers periodic workshops on AI tool integration
Peers actively share tips and use cases for LLMs in team meetings
Internal documentation includes AI usage guidelines
Encouraged to experiment with AI tools and share results with the team

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers stepping through troubleshooting with AI as a collaborative assistant
Uses LLMs for brainstorming solutions before finalizing actions independently
Finds value in both autonomous and guided interactions, depending on task complexity

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an assistant for information retrieval and suggestion
Considers AI a co-worker for routine ticket triage and initial diagnostics
Relies on human judgement for final decision-making, with AI as advisor

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Highly open to integrating AI into daily processes where beneficial
Actively seeks out new AI features or updates in core tools
Willing to pilot new AI-driven workflows and provide feedback to IT leadership

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational AI interfaces for brainstorming and problem-solving
Utilizes command-based interactions for structured tasks like scripting
Appreciates flexibility to switch between modes based on context

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Many troubleshooting scenarios demand deep contextual knowledge of the company’s network setup
User support often requires understanding specific workflows and business processes
AI tools sometimes lack access to detailed, environment-specific information

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for routine diagnostics and common troubleshooting steps
Verifies AI-generated recommendations for critical incidents before applying
Treats AI as a supportive tool rather than a sole decision-maker
Level of trust increases with AI transparency and explainability

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers high automation for repetitive tasks like ticket triage and reporting
Believes human oversight is essential for incident resolution and security decisions
Sees value in AI assisting but not replacing human-driven root cause analysis

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for low-risk, standardized processes
Concerned if automation reduces ability to catch subtle, contextual issues
Prefers manual checks for tasks involving sensitive data or security implications

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Views AI as enhancing core technical skills by enabling focus on complex issues
Believes routine troubleshooting skills may become less critical
Anticipates greater emphasis on analytical and critical thinking abilities

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Learning to interpret and validate AI-generated recommendations
Developing skills in configuring and integrating AI-driven tools
Assuming responsibility for monitoring AI performance and mitigating errors

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Low concern about AI replacing complex troubleshooting or high-context tasks
Recognizes potential for automation to reduce manual workload
Believes human oversight will remain vital for the foreseeable future

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: All AI-driven actions require human approval for critical system changes
Manual review is standard for AI-flagged security incidents
Can intervene and adjust AI recommendations as needed

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Team is increasingly open to piloting AI solutions
Company has begun updating IT policies to include AI tool usage guidelines
Ongoing discussions about balancing automation with job enrichment

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will significantly improve efficiency in IT and network support
Expects AI to create new career opportunities in tech management
Recognizes potential for AI to address skills gaps in the industry
Cautious about risks of over-reliance on automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports responsible AI use with clear accountability measures
Concerned about data privacy and potential biases in AI-driven decisions
Believes transparency and human oversight are essential for ethical AI adoption

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires more accurate AI-driven predictive maintenance tools for networks
Wishes for seamless integration between AI chatbots and ticketing systems
Seeks AI tools capable of real-time, context-aware threat detection

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Anticipates a shift towards more strategic, oversight-focused responsibilities
Expects routine troubleshooting to become largely automated
Foresees increased collaboration with AI systems on proactive issue prevention
Predicts need for continuous upskilling to manage and audit AI tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Develop comprehensive AI adoption and ethics guidelines
Invest in ongoing AI literacy and skills training for IT staff
Pilot and evaluate AI tools before company-wide rollout
Establish clear oversight and accountability for AI-driven processes
